summary: Doc-only audit. Scanned the app target for accessibility-label/hint/value call sites and traced each variable form to its upstream definition. Found 22 sites (16 label, 5 hint, 1 value). Of 9 variable sites: 7 are already-safe (HomeAccessibilityLabels.taskEdit/taskSkip/protocolStepComplete/protocolStepSkip + leadingButtonAccessibilityLabel + continueAccessibilityHint + writeRowAccessibilityHint all route through NSLocalizedString + String.localizedStringWithFormat); 2 are real bypasses (AudioPlaybackButton.accessibilityText, VoiceCaptureButton.accessibilityText return hardcoded English from switch statements). Of 13 literal sites: 11 already-safe (key in Localizable.strings + SwiftUI LocalizedStringKey overload); 1 interpolation-pattern bypass at TrainView line 516 already covered by app-localization-string-interpolation-formatters; 1 acceptable as-is (BuildInfoView label: value pair displays developer fields). Wrote docs/workflows/localization-accessibility-bypass-audit.md. Queued exactly ONE narrow follow-up slice (app-localization-audio-voice-button-accessibility-routing) for the 2 real bypasses. No view code, no resources, no helper code touched in this slice.

contract_status_changes.before: The 2026-05-16 visible-string bypass audit flagged ~10 accessibility var-bypasses as candidates but did not classify the upstream helpers. It was unclear whether HomeAccessibilityLabels / continueAccessibilityHint / writeRowAccessibilityHint already routed through NSLocalizedString.
contract_status_changes.after: Each of the 7 Home/Today/Write accessibility helpers is confirmed to use NSLocalizedString + String.localizedStringWithFormat (Home) or String(localized:) (Write fallback). Only 2 sites are real bypasses, both isolated to DesignSystem audio/voice buttons. The audit catalog and the queued follow-up explicitly scope the fix to those 2 files.

residual_risks: Static scan + helper trace does not verify runtime VoiceOver output. A native-reviewed locale + on-device VoiceOver pass would catch wording errors the LLM-drafted translations might contain.

BuildInfoView line 102's accessibilityLabel('\(label): \(value)') was classified acceptable-as-is. If a future translation needs locale-specific punctuation (e.g., Arabic colon variants), this would surface as a follow-up.

TrainView line 516's accessibilityLabel('\(label), \(value) of 5') is classified as interpolation-pattern bypass. The fix belongs to app-localization-string-interpolation-formatters; this audit does NOT duplicate that work.

The follow-up slice app-localization-audio-voice-button-accessibility-routing will need to add ~7 new keys plus 2 stringsdict-style format strings (Playback error: %@ / Error: %@). All 19 locales must receive LLM-drafted values to preserve parity.
Native review of new keys remains outstanding for every locale. app-localization-native-review-intake is still blocked.
recommended_next_reason: The only real-bypass class surfaced by the accessibility audit. Both files are small (under 100 lines each) and the fix follows the existing HomeAccessibilityLabels pattern, so the slice is low-risk and quickly verifiable.
